text.title: Portfolio Triage Indie Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Portfolio Triage Indie application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Triage report MUST follow a fixed shape: header + per-product row (name, mode, mrr, traffic, verdict, evidence_link, next_action). Free-form prose rejected.
rule.rationale: Fixed shape lets diff-by-week show drift; prose hides drift.

rule.statement: Every per-product row MUST carry an evidence_link (dashboard / Stripe / ticket URL); rows without evidence are rejected.
rule.rationale: Anchored rows survive challenge; unanchored verdicts are folklore.

rule.statement: Report MUST carry a single named owner; collective owner ('me / team / us') is rejected.

rationale.source: Allspaw — On Being a Senior Engineer

rule.rationale: Un-owned reports rot — nobody runs the next-week review.

rule.statement: Each instance MUST carry version + last_reviewed; un-versioned instances are rejected.
rule.rationale: Schema-evolution practice — old instances must re-parse.

rule.statement: Next iteration MUST cite the previous iteration's verdicts and check whether the next-actions executed.
rule.rationale: Without outcome review, verdicts are theatre.
